title: Chapter Twenty-Six: Zhongnan Sect
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Cheong Myeong's blue robe settled over his shoulders like a mantle he had not earned.

The shoulder seams sat wide on Yeon-shin's frame. He adjusted them, feeling the weight of the silk against the white Desolate uniform beneath. A blue robe over white cloth. Rising star of Radiant Demon Wing. The authority to command every white-ranked warrior in the sect, wrapped in another man's coat.

"If anyone asks," Cheong Myeong had said, "tell them the Demon Sword of Radiant Demon Wing acknowledged you. That will prevent most disputes."

"You can use the nickname of the Bewitching Sword Demon as well," Baek Mi-ryeo had added. "Consider it as though I handed you the robe myself."

He had bowed. Something unfamiliar had stirred in his chest -- not pride, exactly. Closer to the weight of being claimed.

Ma Jin's expression had been melancholic. "Regarding the Mount Hua Sect, we'll resolve it. I'm not sure whether you'll return first or the Great Violet Pill incident will conclude."

Hyeon Won-chang had raised his hand carefully. "May I accompany Sir Jeong? I might not be of much help to the leader and the blue-level masters, but it could be different with Sir Jeong."

Ma Jin had thought on it. Nodded.

"Don't forget this has become a mission of Radiant Demon Wing. Observe the fight between the Tyrant Sword Sect and Zhongnan. Consider the impact on civilian life. You may intervene in any way you see fit." A pause. "Try to prioritize your lives first."

"We will follow the command."

Two days south. The Desolate uniforms replaced their Mount Hua garb -- a courtesy to the sect whose face they had bloodied. Won-chang grinned at the characters on his shoulders and pushed his chest out.

"This is the taste."

Yeon-shin said nothing. He wore Cheong Myeong's blue robe over the white and felt the fabric shift against his back with each step.

"White uniform with a blue robe," Won-chang said, glancing sideways. "It suits you."

"It feels awkward."

"You'll adapt quickly, Young Master."

*Zhongnan Mountain. Where Jung-san and Hye have been learning the sword.*

The thought of them opened something behind his ribs. He did not let it rise further.

*Would Hye be doing well?*

---

Zhongnan Mountain filled the southern horizon like a wall.

Steep. Solemn. The peaks cut into the sky with a severity that made Desolate City's fortress look like a child's toy. And at the base, where the trail narrowed between rock faces, four martial artists stood guard.

"These are Zhongnan disciples," Won-chang whispered. "They've come down from the main gate to the foot of the mountain. The conflict with the Tyrant Sword Sect is real."

The guards stepped forward as they approached.

"State your sect, names, and purpose."

"As you can see," Won-chang said, pushing out his shoulder to display the character stitched there. "Desolate Sect."

At the mention of the name, all four guards drew closer. Their expressions hardened. Yeon-shin understood. Desolate Sect existed to police the martial world. The famous sects had never welcomed their presence.

"I am Jeong Yeon-shin of Radiant Demon Wing."

"Hyeon Won-chang, the same."

The guards examined their clothing, their posture, their golden identity tokens -- the Desolate tokens with dwarf-forged wooden patterns plated in gold.

"Do you intend to prove your identity just with clothing?" one snapped. Young. Sharp-eyed. The pride of a famous sect's disciple burned in every word.

Won-chang flared his energy. The air trembled. A subtle wave of true power, spreading outward like a stone dropped in still water.

"It's truly Desolate Sect!" another guard breathed.

But the sharp-eyed one was not impressed. "How dare you flaunt such nonsense at Zhongnan Mountain!"

Won-chang's face reddened. "These guys--"

*The Jeong family.*

The thought came unbidden. Yeon-shin stared at the young guards and saw his brothers' faces. When he and his siblings had spoken of creating their own martial arts, the first expressions on the younger ones' faces had not been bewilderment.

Surprise. And jealousy.

*The fight with the Tyrant Sword Sect is not going well. Their pride is cracking. And now two young strangers arrive claiming to be Desolate warriors.*

"Even if they were truly from Desolate Sect, it's a problem," the fourth guard said, the one who had been silent until now. Force gathered behind his slanted eyes. "How could they send these two to the Great Zhongnan? The reputation of the prestigious Desolate Sect seems to be a thing of the past."

Yeon-shin's hand found the sword wave at his waist.

"Reconsider your words," he said calmly. "You've insulted martial artists from another sect without cause."

The sharp-eyed guard reached for his blade.

Yeon-shin drew first.

The white flash crossed the space between them and stopped. The guard's sword, still half-drawn, vibrated once, then flew from his hand, spinning back through the air toward the mountain.

No one had seen the cut.

"Enough!"

The voice came from above -- jade-clear, enormous in its authority. The bushes trembled in unison. An overwhelming true energy pressed down upon them like the palm of a god.

She descended.

---

Wei Ji Myo-hwa landed between them with the grace of a hawk settling on a branch. About twenty years old. Features sharp enough to cut, set in a face that was transitioning from girlhood to something harder and more composed. Black hair bound with a strange feather decoration. A sky-blue martial uniform that fluttered like a banner. A white sword hanging from her jade belt.

The Sword Dragon of Zhongnan Sect.

*She's strong.*

That was what registered. Not her beauty, not the feather, not the silk -- the energy radiating from her body like heat from a forge, sharpening the air until a falling leaf brushed her robe's hem and split cleanly in two.

*Not inferior to Desolate Sect's blue-level.*

"I am ashamed before the honored guests of Desolate Sect," she said. Her voice was soft. The pressure behind it was not.

Won-chang stood frozen. His jaw had gone slack.

"I am Jeong Yeon-shin, belonging to Radiant Demon Wing," Yeon-shin said. "Hyeon Won-chang, this young master here, is also the same."

"Zhongnan Sect. Wei Ji Myo-hwa."

"Wei Ji Myo-hwa?" Won-chang's voice cracked. "The Sword Dragon of Zhongnan Sect!"

She had beheaded the leader of evil sect forces on the Guanzhong Plain. Already a master who had made her name in the world. A genius who, they said, had descended from heaven by mistake.

"We have rudely come to the Zhongnan main mountain," Yeon-shin said, and clasped his fists. "I am ashamed before the Great Hero Wei."

Not "female hero." Not "lady."

Great Hero.

Her eyes softened. She was so beautiful in that moment that Won-chang's slack jaw became somewhat understandable.

"I'll guide you to our main sect," she said.

As Yeon-shin raised his head from the greeting, a familiar face appeared behind Wei Ji Myo-hwa's shoulder.

Rough features. A cotton robe. A look of absolute shock.

"Yeon-shin?"

His second brother. Jeong Jung-san.

Standing like a man who had seen a ghost.

---
